verses.text: "orando": ver nota de estudio en 3.21 .
" oréis, decid ": la "Oración del Padre Nuestro' fue un modelo a seguir para los discípulos de Jesús.
" oréis, decid ": la "Oración del Padre Nuestro' fue un modelo a seguir para los discípulos de Jesús.

" Y no nos metas en tentación ": esta es una frase difícil en el griego. Dios no tienta a su pueblo ( **Stg 1.13** ), pero dado que la prueba es una parte que produce crecimiento en nuestra vida cristiana ( **Stg 1.2-4** ), ¿deberían los creyentes orar para evitarla? La frase probablemente significa “protégenos durante la tentación”.

11:5 at midnight . . . for a friend is come—The heat in warm countries makes evening preferable to-day for travelling; but "midnight" is everywhere a most unseasonable hour of call, and for that very reason it is here selected.
" un amigo mío ha venido a mí de viaje ": Las normas de hospitalidad en la cultura judía del siglo I exigían que el anfitrión encontrara provisiones adecuadas para el visitante.

11:7 My children are with me in bed - Or, I and my children are in bed; this is Bishop Pearce's translation, and seems to some preferable to the common one. See a like form of speech in 1 Cor 16:11, and in Eph 3:18. However, we may conceive that he had his little children, τα παιδια, in bed with him; and this heightened the difficulty of yielding to his neighbor's request. But if he persevere knocking. (At si ille perseveraverit pulsans).

" por su importunidad" (o "para evitar la vergüenza", o "para que su reputación no se dañe"): la vergüenza también recaería en el hombre que no quería levantarse, ya que la hospitalidad era un requisito comunitario. Dios protege su buen nombre al proveer para su pueblo.

11:9 And (or, therefore) I say unto you, Ask - Be importunate with God, not so much to prevail on him to save you, as to get yourselves brought into a proper disposition to receive that mercy which he is ever disposed to give. He who is not importunate for the salvation of his soul does not feel the need of being saved; and were God to communicate his mercy to such they could not be expected to be grateful for it, as favors are only prized and esteemed in proportion to the sense men have of their necessity and importance.

11:10 If a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father,.... Our Lord illustrates and confirms what he had said before by an instance common among men: the relation between a father and a son is natural, and it is very near; and it is usual for a son, when hungry, and at the proper times of meals, to ask bread of his father: and when he does, will he give him a stone? should he do so, he would show that his heart was as hard, or harder than the stone he gives: or if he ask a fish, will he, for a fish, give him a serpent?

Un pescado y un huevo eran alimentos comunes. Un escorpión se parece a un huevo cuando se enrosca en la forma de una bola para atraer a su presa. Dar una serpiente o un escorpión sería una broma cruel, sustituyendo algo mortal por una buena comida. Ningún padre amoroso haría tal cosa.

Un pescado y un huevo eran alimentos comunes. Un escorpión se parece a un huevo cuando se enrosca en la forma de una bola para atraer a su presa. Dar una serpiente o un escorpión sería una broma cruel, sustituyendo algo mortal por una buena comida. Ningún padre amoroso haría tal cosa.

" dará el Espíritu Santo": el paralelo de Mateo ( **Mateo 7.11** ) dice “ dará buenas cosas .” El Espíritu Santo es el mayor regalo para los creyentes, proporcionando guía, poder y el sello de la salvación. Lucas da especial preeminencia a la obra del Espíritu Santo en Lucas y Hechos.
"un demonio de un hombre que no podía hablar": los demonios a veces causan enfermedad o discapacidad física.

Satanás: en griego Beelzebú (“Baal, el príncipe”), el nombre de un dios cananeo que más tarde se utilizó como nombre para Satanás. Los judíos se burlaban del nombre, llamándolo Beelzebub (“señor de las moscas”).

11:16 But he knowing their thoughts,.... Being God omniscient, said unto them; the following parables, as they are called in Mark 3:23 or proverbial expressions, very pertinent to the purpose, and sufficient to set aside the base calumnies of the Pharisees: every kingdom divided against itself, is brought to desolation; in process of time, division will end in destruction; and as it does in the kingdoms of the world, of which there have been fatal instances, so it would in the kingdom of Satan, was there in it a division, which the calumny of the Pharisees supposes: and an house divided against an house, falleth.

" él, conociendo los pensamientos de ellos" : ver **5.22** ; **7.40** ; **9.47** . • " Todo reino dividido contra sí mismo" : la primera respuesta de Jesús a la acusación fue que no podía estar trabajando para Satanás; estaba derribando el reino de Satanás al expulsar demonios.

11:18 And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils,.... Which is what the Pharisees charged him with; in the Greek copies, and so in the Arabic and Ethiopic versions it is read, "by Beelzebul", and so in the preceding verses; See Gill on Matt 10:25. By whom do your sons cast them out? by whose help? or in whose name? for the Jews pretended to cast out devils, and to heal those that were possessed with them; which they did sometimes, by making use of the names of the patriarchs, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and sometimes of the name of Solomon: Josephus (q) speaks of many in his time, who had this power of healing;

11:19 But if I with the, finger of God,.... The power of God, referring to Exo 8:19 and so the Cabalistic Jews (r) explain it, "the finger is one of the five in the hand, and is that finger which works by the power of Elohim;'' it is the same with the Spirit of God; See Gill on Matt 12:28 which is often called the hand of the Lord, Ezek 1:3. (r) R. Mosch in Sepher Hashem, apud Cabal. Denudata. T.
"el Reino de Dios ha llegado a vosotros": los exorcismos de Jesús eran evidencia de que el reino de Dios estaba presente.

El hombre fuerte es Satanás; el hombre más fuerte es Jesús. Los exorcismos de Jesús demostraron que él estaba derrotando y saqueando la propiedad de Satanás: la gente mantenida cautiva por él (cp. **Is 49.24-26** ).

El hombre fuerte es Satanás; el hombre más fuerte es Jesús. Los exorcismos de Jesús demostraron que él estaba derrotando y saqueando la propiedad de Satanás: la gente mantenida cautiva por él (cp. **Is 49.24-26** ).
" El que no es conmigo, contra mí es" : ver nota de estudio en 9.50 .

11:24 And when he cometh, he findeth it swept and garnished. In Matthew it is also said to be "empty"; and so it is read here in the Arabic version; and in the Ethiopic version, "empty of men": but rather the sense is, that he found it empty of all goodness, notwithstanding all the sweeping and garnish of an outward reformation. The Persic version renders it, "heated and prepared"; heated with wrath and fury against Christ, and his Gospel, and so was prepared and fitted to be a proper habitation for Satan;

11:25 And it came to pass as he spoke these things,.... That is, as Christ spoke, or "had finished these sayings", as the Persic version expresses it, before related, in vindication of himself and his miracles, from the blasphemy of the Scribes and Pharisees to their entire confusion, and had delivered the above parable concerning the unclean spirit, which had a particular regard to them: a certain woman of the company: observing the miracle he had wrought, in casting out a devil, and being affected with his discourse, in which he so fully cleared himself, and so strongly confuted his enemies, and set them forth in so just a light: lift up her voice, and said unto him, aloud, in the hearing of all the people, and with great earnestness and…
" toma otros siete espíritus" : siete puede indicar totalidad, lo que significa que el hombre estaba completamente bajo su poder, o simplemente podría indicar una fuerza abrumadora.

11:27 as he spake these things, a . . . woman of the company—of the multitude, the crowd. A charming little incident and profoundly instructive. With true womanly feeling, she envies the mother of such a wonderful Teacher. Well, and higher and better than she had said as much before her (Luke 1:28, Luke 1:42); and our Lord is far from condemning it. He only holds up—as "blessed rather"—the hearers and keepers of God's word; in other words, the humblest real saint of God.
" Antes bienaventurados" : Jesús nuevamente afirmó que nuestra vida espiritual y nuestra relación con Dios son más importantes que la ascendencia física y las relaciones familiares (cp. **8.21** ).

La señal de Jonás podría referirse a la resurrección; el tiempo de Jonás en el pez fue una señal para los ninivitas de que Dios lo había enviado, como el tiempo de Jesús en la tumba (ver **Mateo 12.40** ). Podría simplemente significar que tanto Jonás como Jesús predicaron la advertencia de Dios de arrepentirse (ver **Lucas 11.32** ).

La señal de Jonás podría referirse a la resurrección; el tiempo de Jonás en el pez fue una señal para los ninivitas de que Dios lo había enviado, como el tiempo de Jesús en la tumba (ver **Mateo 12.40** ). Podría simplemente significar que tanto Jonás como Jesús predicaron la advertencia de Dios de arrepentirse (ver **Lucas 11.32** ).
Sabá era un reino en el sur de Arabia. La reina viajó una gran distancia para escuchar la sabiduría de Salomón ( **1 Reyes 10.1-13,** **2 Crónicas 9.1-12** ).

11:32 The light of the body is the eye,.... The Vulgate Latin and all the eastern versions read "the light of thy body is thine eye". The sense is, that as the eye gives light, to the body, and the several members of it, by which they are guided and directed; so the understanding is the light of the soul, and the guide to all the powers and faculties of it; therefore when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light: as when the eye is free from vicious humours, and its sight is clear, the whole body reaps the advantage of it, and is perfectly illuminated by it;
Nadie enciende una lámpara y luego la esconde: Ver nota de estudio en 8.16-17 .
11:34 The light of the body is the eye - Or, the eye is the lamp of the body. See on Matt 6:22 (note), etc. The 35th and 36th verses are wanting in some MSS., and are variously read in others.
" le rogó un fariseo que comiese con él" : ver nota de estudio en 7.36 .
la ceremonia de lavado de manos requerida por la costumbre judía: los fariseos habían desarrollado, elaborados rituales de lavado para asegurar la pureza ceremonial (ver **Marcos 7.1-5** ).

Los fariseos tenían reglas meticulosas para la limpieza ceremonial de tazas y utensilios para mantener la pureza religiosa. Jesús los acusó de limpiar solo el exterior de la taza (siguiendo reglas externas) pero dejando el interior . . . sucio (manteniendo la injusticia en sus corazones).

11:40 Did not he that made that which is without - Did not the maker of the dish form it so, both outwardly and inwardly, as to answer the purpose for which it was made? And can it answer this purpose without being clean in the inside as well as on the outside? God has made you such, both as to your bodies and souls, as he intended should show forth his praise; but can you think that the purpose of God can be accomplished by you while you only attend to external legal purifications, your hearts being full of rapine and wickedness?
" dad limosna de lo que tenéis" : los actos de amor revelan la justicia interna.

" diezmáis la menta, y la ruda, y toda hortaliza" : diezmar era requerido por la ley ( **Lev 27.30-33,** **Num 18.21-32,** **Deut 14.22-29,** **2 Cron 31.5-12** ). Los fariseos eran meticulosos a la hora de asegurarse de que todo se diezmaba correctamente, pero ignoraban los asuntos más importantes: la justicia y el amor de Dios.

Los ancianos de las sinagogas tenían asientos especiales de honor. • Los saludos respetuosos eran formas honorables de dirigirse a las personas de mayor rango social y religioso (como dirigirse a un juez como “su señoría”).

" sepulcros que no se ven" : tocar una tumba o un cadáver hacía a un judío ceremonialmente impuro. Jesús acusó a los líderes religiosos de ser una influencia contaminante en lugar de purificadora. Esta fue una acusación fuertemente ofensiva ( **11.45** ).
" uno de los intérpretes de la ley" : los expertos en la ley religiosa estaban estrechamente alineados con los fariseos y compartían el mismo celo por mantener la ley de Moisés.

" con cargas que no pueden llevar" : esto se refiere a la tradición oral con todos sus detalles intrincados sobre vivir de acuerdo con la torah (la instrucción de Dios en los libros de Moisés). La tradición oral impuso una gran carga sobre el pueblo judío.

11:47 ye build, &c.—Out of pretended respect and honor, they repaired and beautified the sepulchres of the prophets, and with whining hypocrisy said, "If we had been in the days of our fathers, we should not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets," while all the time they "were witnesses to themselves that they were the children of them that killed the prophets" (Matt 23:29-30); convicting themselves daily of as exact a resemblance in spirit and character to the very classes over whose deeds they pretended to mourn, as child to parent.
Mataron a los profetas: ver **1 Reyes 19.10** , **14** ; **Ne 9.26** ; **Jer 2.30;** **26.20-24** . Lucas presenta a Jesús como “El Profeta Sufriente” **Lucas 4.22-24** .
La cita no es explícitamente del Antiguo Testamento.

11:50 all . . . required of this generation—As it was only in the last generation of them that "the iniquity of the Amorites was full" (Gen 15:16), and then the abominations of ages were at once completely and awfully avenged, so the iniquity of Israel was allowed to accumulate from age to age till in that generation it came to the full, and the whole collected vengeance of Heaven broke at once over its devoted head. In the first French Revolution the same awful principle was exemplified, and Christendom has not done with it yet.

" desde la sangre de Abel hasta la sangre de Zacarías" : el asesinato de Abel por su hermano Caín fue el primer asesinato registrado en el Antiguo Testamento ( **Gn 4.8** ). La lapidación de Zacarías, hijo de Joiada ( **2 Cron 24.20-22** ) fue el último, ya que Crónicas es el último libro en la Biblia hebrea.
" la llave de la ciencia" : ver **Mateo 16.19** ; **23.13** .

11:53 Exceedingly vivid and affecting. They were stung to the quick—and can we wonder?—yet had not materials for the charge they were preparing against Him. provoke him, &c.—"to harass Him with questions." Next: Luke Chapter 12

11:54 Laying wait for him, and seeking to catch something out of his mouth, that they might accuse him: The scribes and Pharisees move from theological objection to forensic ambush. *Enedreuontes* (laying wait) is hunters' vocabulary. The verse closes the conflict-cycle of Luke 11 and prepares for the broader hostility that culminates at the cross.
